# opnsense_dnsmasq_host (Data Source)

Look up an existing Dnsmasq host entry on OPNsense by UUID without importing it into Terraform management.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "opnsense_dnsmasq_host" "example" {
  id = "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012"
}

output "dnsmasq_host_id" {
  value = data.opnsense_dnsmasq_host.example.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) UUID to look up.

### Read-Only

- `aliases` (String) Legacy aliases for this host entry.
- `client_id` (String) DHCP client identifier.
- `cnames` (String) Canonical name aliases for this host entry.
- `comments` (String) Additional comments for this host entry.
- `description` (String) Description for this host entry.
- `domain` (String) Domain for this host entry.
- `host` (String) Hostname for this Dnsmasq host entry.
- `hwaddr` (String) Hardware or MAC address values.
- `ignore` (Boolean) Ignore DHCP requests from this host.
- `ip` (String) IP address or addresses for this host entry.
- `lease_time` (Number) DHCP lease time for this host entry.
- `local` (Boolean) Serve this host locally without forwarding.
- `set_tag` (String) DHCP tag to set for this host.
